reviews.name: Ramanan Duraisamy
reviews.text: After experiencing their excellent catering at a recent corporate event, I had to visit the restaurant myself. The breakfast was outstanding and the flavors are truly authentic. Chennai Tiffins is definitely a specialist in both large-scale events and high-quality dining. A must-visit for any South Indian food lover!

reviews.name: Aniket Jain
reviews.text: Food and service both has improved. New menu items as well.

reviews.name: ash
reviews.text:
We’ve been coming here as a family for breakfast for a long time, and the food is consistently excellent. It has always been our go-to spot.

Unfortunately, during our last visit( feb,8), we had an uncomfortable experience with a staff member. When I asked if plates could be cleared from our table during the buffet breakfast, I was told they prefer reusing plates. I apologized, as I wasn’t aware of this policy.

After that, I overheard the staff member mocking us by saying each person used four plates, which was not true—we were three people and used five plates in total. The restaurant was also not crowded at the time, so the comment felt unnecessary.

While I understand policies around reducing waste, being mocked made us feel embarrassed and unwelcome.

title: Renaissance Reno Downtown Hotel & Spa
description: Warm rooms & suites in a contemporary riverfront property offering dining & a pool.
address: One Lake St, Reno, NV 89501
hotelDescription:
On the shores of the Truckee River, this modern hotel in a lively area with restaurants and bars is a 10-minute walk from the Nevada Museum of Art, and 3 miles from both Reno-Sparks Convention Center and Reno-Tahoe International Airport.
 Warm rooms provide Wi-Fi access (fee) and flat-screen TVs, plus minifridges and coffeemakers. The suites add sitting areas and floor-to-ceiling windows; some have city views and separate living rooms. Room service is available.
 Valet parking and an airport shuttle (on request) are free. Other amenities include a Mediterranean restaurant, and a sports bar, along with an outdoor pool, a hot tub and a gym.

title: Wyndham Visalia
description: Contemporary lodging near a golf course, with a cafe/bar, free WiFi & an airport shuttle.
address: 9000 W Airport Dr, Visalia, CA 93277
hotelDescription:
This contemporary lodging is a 3-minute walk from Visalia Airport and a 3-minute drive to Valley Oaks Golf Course.
 Modern rooms come with desks and flat-screen TVs, plus pull-out sofas and coffeemakers. There's also free WiFi and high-pressure showerheads, plus kids 19 and under stay free with an adult. Suites add separate living spaces, minifridges and microwaves.
 Amenities include a restaurant and bar offering Californian cuisine (where kids 12 and under eat free) and both indoor and outdoor pools, plus a fitness center, full conference facilities and a free airport/Amtrak shuttle.

title: The Grand Hotel
description: Sophisticated lodging with an outdoor pool, plus free Wi-Fi, breakfast & cocktail hour.
address: 865 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087
hotelDescription:
A 6-minute walk from the Sunnyvale Civic Center, this modern hotel is a 14-minute drive from Mineta San Jose International Airport.
 The bright, airy rooms offer free Wi-Fi, desks and 24-hour room service. Suites add separate living spaces, fireplaces and whirlpool tubs. There are also private bungalows with full kitchens.
 Complimentary perks include a breakfast buffet, evening drinks and snacks, and limo service within a 3-mile radius (airport limo service is available for a surcharge). There's also a heated outdoor pool and a hot tub.

reviews.text:
I booked 4 beded  3 rooms in Chenna Guest House on dated 02.10.2025 with talked to Mr Agarwal who is owner of this hotel, as we  12 senior citizens are going on pilgrimage of religious  places of South India. As per program we are reaching Chennai in the evening of 25.10.2025. I had a very very bitter experience at the Chenna Guest House.
First as we reached reception I told  the manager of the guest house to accept the amount of money online as we have not much cash amount  but he denied it in very rudely manner. When I told him that can I request Mr Agarwal for the same his reply was very shocking,  He told me who is the Agarwal I am the manager here and Agarwal will do as I say.
We shifted in rooms and after that we went for dinner. After dinner when we went into the rooms we noticed that the blankets were not in sufficient quantity and AC's are also not working. I called the manager on the hotel phone as there was no availability of intercom facilities. I was shocked to listen to the manager's reply he told me 'All AC's are working and sufficient blankets are there don't disturb me again if you have any problem come down and don't call me. I sent my relative to collect blankets but he did not support He said in six blankets 12 persons can adjust, when we told him that there we are ladies and gents of different relationship and not possible to adjust in one blanket, his reply was very surprising he said all gents and ladies relative can sleep together, when we oppose his statement he came in front of reception and started men handling. He didn't stop here and he threatened us that his relative is the minister and he has an approach in the police and can call the police and arrest us all.
We were afraid and took the decision to leave the hotel. I called the owner of the hotel Mr Agarwal but he also supported his manager. When we requested him to  refund the amount given by me, he denied my request. At last we all senior citizens left the hotel at midnight and shifted to another hotel.
My request to all please think hundreds of times before booking this hotel. If you want safety and your security then don't book this hotel.

reviews.name: Debisree Ray
reviews.text:
I ordered dinner from Saravana Bhavan through Uber Eats. As a well-known South Indian chain with a long-standing legacy and multiple locations across the country, I’ve previously tried their outlets in New York and New Jersey. This was my first experience with their Bay Area location.

I ordered a rava masala dosa along with kesari halwa for dessert. The rava dosa had a nice crunch, which I always appreciate, but the potato masala inside was quite bland. The chutneys tasted fresh but lacked depth, and the sambar was fairly basic as well. Overall, it felt a bit underwhelming compared to the East Coast locations, which I personally enjoyed more.

That said, it’s still a decent option if you’re craving lighter, carb-heavy, vegetarian South Indian food. The highlight of the meal was definitely the dessert. The kesari halwa — a semolina-based pudding infused with saffron was moderately sweet, pleasantly sticky, and studded with raisins. It was simple but satisfying.

Overall, a decent experience, though not my favorite Saravana Bhavan location.

reviews.name: Kimo Rongphar
reviews.text:
I booked a room in Just Guest House through Ixigo to stay for a night as I had an early morning flight to catch. When I arrived outside a person was ready to assist me in carrying my luggage to the reception. The process for check-in was carried out carefully and efficiently as their only required Name and address along with your Identification (Aadhar in this case) and I was lead to my room. The receptionist is very helpful as making sure queries I had were answered and any requests made were carried out smoothly. The room I got was enough for a single person to stay in. There was a enough room for a single person to stay which was air conditioned and also has a fan too. The bathroom was spacious with a bucket and mug provided and shower option available. The Guest house provides towel and soap for bathing and anyone wanting to travel light and also freshen up can use it effectively. A door hangar is also available in the room to hang clothes etc. The place does not have meal options but local south indian meals and even some fast food options are available at walkable distance if a person chooses to explore otherwise one can always choose food orders (Zomato, swiggy etc) to have a meal. Overall a wonderful overnight stay experience and a great service. Highly recommended for solo or even duo travellers having an early morning flight. Satisfied with the stay and looking forward to next time
Note: Specifically for people staying in for early morning flights, check-out option is not even if done in early morning. Paid Taxi option is available for a fixed price of 300/- the number can be obtained from the reception. It is adviced to check the booking prices In Uber before making the decision decide.
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Mr. Kimo Rongphar,

Thank you very much for your top rating and for taking the time to share such a detailed and thoughtful review about your stay at Just Guest House, Chennai Airport.

We are truly happy to know that you had a comfortable overnight stay and appreciated our check-in process, room facilities, cleanliness, and staff support. Your kind words are very encouraging for our entire team and motivate us to continue providing the best service to our guests.

We are glad our location, budget-friendly pricing, and early-morning travel support suited your needs. Thank you also for highlighting the nearby food options and transport details, which will be helpful for future guests.

We look forward to welcoming you again on your next visit to Chennai.

Warm regards,
Selva
Just Guest House
Chennai Airport

reviews.name: Katy Kondo
reviews.text:
We were desperate to book a place due to the dense fog. We are loyal to Motel 6 because we’ve had good experiences in the past. The man who checked me in was nice enough, but seemed confused and took a full minute to locate our room on the map. The stairway to the room felt like a labyrinthine fire hazard.

The lights were difficult to operate, and even with all of the lights on, the room was still dim. It sounded like we were sleeping on the freeway. It was cold, even with the “heat” on — you could feel the cold coming in through the windows. The cigarette-burned blanket was so thin, we had to wear hats to sleep.

The bathroom door was coming off, and the door would get stuck in the jamb. When you pried it off, bits of the door would fall off. And the sink looked like someone had just shaved and no one cleaned the hair. The towels were thin and not very absorbent. No shampoo or conditioner, just soap.

Overall, this place is passable in a pinch, but would not recommend if you have any other options.

reviews.name: surendhar kumar
reviews.text:
A Peaceful Sanctuary in the Heart of Auroville

My stay at Atithi Griha Guest House was nothing short of extraordinary. Nestled within the serene Bharati Nivas campus, this guest house offers a perfect blend of simplicity, heritage, and soulful tranquility. From the moment I arrived, I felt a deep sense of peace—the kind that only Auroville can offer.

The architecture is timeless, with traditional Indian elements that instantly ground you in the cultural essence of the space. The rooms are neat, comfortable, and thoughtfully designed to reflect the spirit of minimalism and sustainability.

What truly sets this place apart is the warmth and grace of the staff. Every interaction was filled with genuine kindness and care. Their quiet attentiveness and readiness to help made my experience seamless and deeply personal.

Waking up to the sound of birds, walking through tree-lined paths, and enjoying the nourishing breakfast each morning felt like a spiritual retreat. It’s not just a guest house—it’s a sanctuary for the soul.

I wholeheartedly recommend Atithi Griha to anyone seeking rest, reflection, and a deeper connection with Auroville’s ethos. Thank you for such a divine and memorable stay.

reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Pratik
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We're sorry to hear about your experience and are actively working on several improvements across the guest house, including maintenance, cleanliness, and infrastructure.

However, we do want to clarify a few factual inaccuracies in your review:

There are not one but two restaurants within 1 km of Swagatham Guest House, located at the Auroville Visitors’ Centre — it is a main spot among guests for fresh, well-rated meals.

We are also closely located to several shops and cafés, not 6 km away as mentioned, please find them here : https://share.google/AOnQqrdL0LjNNRh1v 

Regarding your concerns about insects and power cuts — being situated in a forested area, occasional power fluctuations and encounters with nature do occur, but we’ve taken concrete steps to address both through upgraded pest control and electrical backup systems. However, the only thing that is not working during power cuts is our AC and that’s get turned on if there’s a power cuts for a longer period of time with the help of our backup generators. We are working on the pest control the best we can.

We are not a restaurant we only serve complimentary breakfast to our customers. The in-house breakfast is modest and simple such as simple south Indian cuisine that is offered to our guests.

We’ve taken the rest of your comments seriously and have already started improving the quality and variety of offerings.

Swagatham is in the midst of a transition with a new management team. We’re actively enhancing our spaces, services, and hospitality. We hope you’ll consider revisiting us in the future to experience the changes.

Warm regards,
Team Swagatham
Swagatham Guest House, Auroville

 Simple rooms with wood furnishings and a bohemian vibe offer safes; some have fans or air-conditioning. Family rooms sleep up to 5 people, and some include bunk beds, stained-glass windows and porches. Bathrooms are shared (cold water only) or en suite.

reviews.name: Chandana Machiraju
reviews.text:
The most fraudulent hostel in pondicherry. Latha, the owner of the hostel chain charged me Rs 2000 more per month compared to other girls just because I spoke in English and was from another state. Instead of 10000 per month for a non AC room, I used to pay 12,350 and I got to know this only while vacating. She even charged me for one month extra rent to "reserve" the room when there were many more rooms available and when I pointed it out, she spoke arrogantly and asked me to vacate if I want.
It's truly a scam because hostels are supposed to display a board outside but these people don't so clearly they are trying to evade taxes.
And do not get me started on how noisy all the girls are at midnight, laughing, partying and disturbing everyone. The owner doesn't care despite several complaints. Everyone comes to study but they just sit and chitchat.
The food provided is horrible, I used to leave it and eat outside on most days. The chutneys are watery, the curries don't have any vegetables in them. They charge so much but use the cheapest ingredients. Also, the food was never delivered on time in the morning so I used to end up missing breakfast or packing lunch. The akka also didn't clean well so when I complained, the maid was changed so that was a good thing. The bathrooms were not cleaned properly.
The rooms are so small that they made us all feel claustrophobic. Charging so much rent for such a tiny space and the worst facilities is nothing but a scam.

reviews.name: mariyam hadi
reviews.text:
I paid ₹10,000 in advance for an 11-day stay at Rosepark in Pondicherry, planned for a B.Ed. examination. Upon our arrival, the contact person, Lata, did not respond to our calls, leaving us waiting until evening before we had to book another room elsewhere.

Despite Lata's promise to refund the money, she was unresponsive to numerous calls, and it took 250 attempts over 11 days for her to finally return the money. It seems Lata, along with her agent Kalaivani, had taken money from around 100 students, and it is unclear if all of them received their refunds.

Due to this fraudulent behavior, I do not recommend Rosepark. Be cautious of dealing with Lata and Kalaivani to avoid similar experiences.

reviews.text:
would like to provide honest feedback regarding my recent stay at your hotel.
Unfortunately, the experience was far below expectations. The hotel staff was not friendly or welcoming toward customers. Hospitality plays a major role in customer satisfaction, and the lack of courteous behavior directly affects the hotel’s reputation and revenue.
The rooms are extremely small and poorly planned. There is no proper seating arrangement and no adequate facility for hanging clothes. When using the bathroom, guests are forced to leave their clothes on the bed, and after bathing, they must again pick up their clothes from the bed to change. This is highly inconvenient and uncomfortable.
Additionally, the presence of cockroaches in the room is completely unacceptable and raises serious concerns about cleanliness and hygiene.
Furthermore, the television and internet services do not function properly, causing further inconvenience during the stay.
I strongly recommend immediate improvements in staff behavior, room planning, cleanliness standards, and technical services. Enhancing these aspects is essential for customer satisfaction, positive reviews, and ultimately for protecting and increasing the hotel’s revenue.
I hope the management will take corrective action at the earliest.

reviews.text:
I had an amazing experience staying at Greens Suite (Business Class Hotel) during my visit to Chennai. The hotel was extremely clean and well-maintained, which made my stay very comfortable from start to finish.
The staff were exceptionally friendly and helpful — they made me feel welcomed throughout my visit. I especially want to mention the young woman at the reception who was incredibly supportive and attentive. Her assistance truly made a positive difference to my stay.
The complimentary breakfast was delicious with good variety, and I really enjoyed starting my mornings there.
The only small drawback is the location visibility. Although the hotel is very close to the railway station, the pocket road leading to it is not easily noticeable from the main road. If you’re coming by taxi or car, it can be easy to miss the turn.
Overall, Greens Suite offers great value with excellent service, friendly staff, and a pleasant atmosphere. I would definitely recommend it to anyone visiting Chennai.

reviews.name: Shreyash Deokar
reviews.text:
This was by far the worst hotel experience I have ever had. Please do not book this hotel even by mistake.

From the moment we arrived, the staff behaved in an extremely rude and unprofessional manner. They were not helpful at all and showed no interest in resolving even basic issues. It felt like guests were treated as a burden rather than customers.

The rooms were in terrible condition. There was a strong, unpleasant smell throughout the room. Cleanliness was clearly not maintained. Basic facilities were missing — no proper chairs, no table, no drinking water provided, and poor air ventilation. The room felt suffocating and uncomfortable. There was no sense of safety or security.

For the amount of money being charged, the service provided was absolutely unacceptable. It honestly felt like a scam. There was zero comfort, zero hospitality, and zero accountability.

If the owner happens to read this review, please understand that customer satisfaction is not optional in the hospitality industry. Train your staff properly, improve room maintenance, ensure cleanliness, and provide basic amenities. Charging money without providing even minimum standards of service will eventually damage your reputation permanently.

I strongly do not recommend this hotel to anyone. Save your money and choose a place that respects its guests.

reviews.name: Inspirit In
reviews.text:
I love Andhra meals with gun-powder, ghee, gongura chutney etc. Went to National Lodge after seeing the youtube videos. The building and the room layout was a bit of surprise and I wasn't sure what to expect. But once the food got served, I was blown away. We were in Room C and there was an elderly gentleman serving food. He was treating us like his close relatives visiting his house. He explained why every item is special here and almost force fed us. It was so refreshing to see this elderly gentleman loving his job and giving his best.
I have complained in the past about a lot of other eateries not having good service in spite of having good food. These owners can just send their staff to this place for training.

Loved the food & service. Won't complain about the crowded streets & cramped rooms as long as they serve tasty food and have such wonderful staff. I prefer such places instead of swanky restaurants with staff who take orders like robots. Will definitely visit again!

description: Bright hotel with laid-back rooms & suites, plus a relaxed restaurant & an exercise room.
address: No: 14, Meeran Sahib St, Mount Road, Chintadripet, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600002, India
hotelDescription:
This bright, colourful hotel is 1.7 km from a train station, 3.1 km from the bustling Marina Beach and 4.4 km from the 7th-century Kapaleeswarar Temple.
 The laid-back, subdued rooms feature satellite TV and free Wi-Fi. Suites and upgraded rooms add sitting areas and air-conditioning. Room service is available 24/7.
 There's a relaxed vegetarian restaurant. Other amenities include an exercise room and a lobby lounge with a TV. Valet parking is available, and pets are welcome.

hotelAds.title: Hotel Mallika | Budget Hotel in Chennai

reviews.name: phullie
reviews.text: The room isn't horrible, quite decent for its cost in comparison with other rooms across Chennai. That being said, I had asked about an upgrade, as I was looking for a room with a window and more fresh air, and was quoted a specific price. The day after the guy at the counter had increased the price. Around 1000₹ you could find better rooms in that area. There were other issues as well, leaking water from the sink kept the room floor quite moist, to say the least. The neighbors are easily heard from nearby rooms and to put it gently, have been making sounds which you wouldn't want to hear. I stayed only for 1 night as a traveler, crossing India with a motorbike. Booked it online so I didn't mind all the small details and faults, checked out early and in general is went smoothly. That being said, it's difficult to recommend such a place, only if you're looking for a budget room for around 500₹.

reviews.text:
I recently stayed at The Chennai Inn in Zamin Pallavaram and overall it was a decent experience for the price. The hotel has a good location close to the main road and is easy to reach from the airport and local spots.  ￼

What I liked:
✔ Friendly and helpful staff
✔ Clean rooms with necessary amenities
✔ Comfortable for short stays or budget trips
✔ Close to transport and shops nearby  ￼

What could improve:
⚠ Some guests mentioned cleanliness and hygiene could be better, especially bathrooms.  ￼
⚠ Food is not included, so you need to find restaurants nearby.  ￼
⚠ A few reviews note issues with facilities like water heating and cockroaches.  ￼

Overall:
A budget-friendly hotel with good location and decent service — great for short stays or business trips. If you’re looking for luxury, this isn’t it, but for value for money it’s solid.

reviews.name: DEEPAK KUMAR
reviews.text:
This place should honestly not be called a hotel. My stay at Hotel White Park, Chennai was a complete nightmare.
There was NO WATER in the taps when I checked in. I informed the staff immediately, and shockingly, the problem was not fixed even after 1 hour. Imagine paying for a room where you can’t even wash your hands or use the toilet properly. Absolutely unacceptable.
The staff was rude and completely uncooperative. Instead of taking responsibility or apologizing, they acted like this kind of service failure is normal. Zero professionalism.
There was no intercom phone in the room, so every time I needed help, I had to physically go down and chase the staff. In 2026, this is beyond ridiculous.
On top of all this, no complimentary water bottles were provided. Not even basic hospitality standards were met.
This hotel is a disgrace to the hospitality industry. I strongly warn everyone: DO NOT STAY HERE unless you enjoy suffering, wasting money, and being treated like your comfort doesn’t matter.

reviews.name: Devanand A A
reviews.text:
I recently had the pleasure of staying at this hotel in Pondicherry, and overall, it was a very satisfying experience. The room was well maintained, clean, and comfortable throughout our stay. One of the highlights was the wonderful balcony, which turned out to be incredibly useful and added a relaxing charm to the entire experience. The bed and overall room setup were good, making our stay comfortable.
There are a couple of areas that could be improved, such as the bathroom door lock and additional storage space. Although a wardrobe was provided, a bit more storage would make longer stays even more convenient. However, these were very minor concerns compared to the overall experience.
What truly stood out was the exceptional quality of service. Both the morning and evening staff were extremely courteous, patient, and professional. We had a slight confusion regarding a payment transfer, but the staff handled the situation calmly and patiently without any inconvenience to us. Their hospitality was genuinely top notch and made us feel welcomed and valued.
Because of the outstanding service and overall comfort, I would definitely consider staying at this hotel again whenever I visit Pondicherry. Highly recommended for anyone looking for warm hospitality and a pleasant stay.

reviews.name: Knath 797
reviews.text:
Shivam PG – Honest Review (Important for New Joiners)
Shivam PG is good in terms of location, food, and overall stay. Chennai food is nice, and daily living is comfortable.
But the main issue is money handling and refund clarity.
At the time of joining, they said:
If I pay ₹12,000, they will return it at the time of leaving after cutting around ₹1,000.
I trusted this and stayed.
Later, when I stayed 10 extra days, the calculation was not clearly explained:
From my ₹12,000, ₹4,666 was cut
Maintenance charge: ₹3,000
Current bill: ₹850 for 40 days
Because of this, I faced a much bigger loss than what was originally promised.
There was no clear written policy or proper explanation about how refunds and charges are calculated.
Warning for New Joiners:
When you join:
Get the refund policy in written form
Ask for a clear breakup of advance, maintenance, and electricity charges
Do not trust only verbal promises
When leaving, make sure everything is documented
Summary:
Food: Good
Stay: Good
Location: Good
Money & Refund Transparency: Poor
Overall, the PG is okay to live in, but be very careful about money matters.
If you don’t keep everything documented, you may face a loss like I did.

reviews.name: Dasari Bhasita
reviews.text:
We stayed at La Villa Creole Home Stay and overall had a pleasant experience. The room was very beautiful and thoughtfully designed, with a lovely vintage chair where you can sit back and relax — it really added to the charm and ambience of the place.

The washroom was a bit tiny, and the room has thin walls, as we could hear voices from the next room. Also, the starting stretch of the road leading to the property had a strong unpleasant smell, which was quite off-putting, but thankfully it’s only for a short distance — once you cross that part, the rest of the road is absolutely fine.

On the plus side, the location is excellent. The stay is at a walkable distance from Rock Beach and is almost centrally located — close to the French Colony, Vinayagar Temple, Kamatchi Hotel, and several cafés and bakeries, making it very convenient to get around.

We had booked a hut stay but were provided with an AC room instead. We were also informed that they are planning to open a kitchen within the premises soon, which would be a great addition.

The manager deserves a special mention — he was very polite and helpful, especially since we had a late check-in. He’s from Hyderabad as well, which made the interaction even more pleasant.

Overall, it’s a good stay for the ambience and easy commute to nearby attractions. A nice option if location and vibe matter to you.

Overall, I would rate the stay 3/5 — the price does not quite match the facilities and services offered.

title: Hotel Atithi
subTitle: ஹோட்டல் அதிதி
description: Warm rooms & suites in a relaxed hotel with an outdoor pool, rooftop dining & breakfast.
address: 126, Sardar Vallabbhai Patel Salai, Heritage Town, Puducherry, 605001, India
hotelDescription:
This relaxed hotel, set in a contemporary building, lies 1 km from Promenade Beach and Vinayagar Temple, a Hindu pilgrimage site, and 2.1 km from Puducherry railway station.
 Unassuming, warmly decorated rooms feature Wi-Fi, flat-screen TVs, and tea and coffeemaking facilities, plus minibars. Suites add sitting areas. Room service is available 24/7.
 Breakfast is available. There are 2 bars and 2 international restaurants, including a rooftop BBQ option, plus a coffee shop. There's also an outdoor pool, a fitness room and a spa.

reviews.name: shilpa santhosh
reviews.text:
Ram Guest House feels more like staying in a traditional old house rather than a typical hotel, which gives it a certain charm. I’m staying here for the second time, and on both occasions the staff have been very friendly and welcoming.

The building is old but solid, strong, and well maintained. Despite its vintage character, the facilities are fairly modern, with amenities like AC and a water heater available. The ambience is peaceful and comfortable for a relaxed stay. However, the rooms are not fully soundproof, so it may not be the best choice for couples or guests seeking a high level of privacy. Overall, a good and practical option, especially given its convenient location near White Town.
